top-story-1:
Russia chose military escalation over any prospect of an Easter truce, launching one of the largest coordinated bombardments of the war against Ukraine on Saturday with hundreds of drones and missiles.<sup>[1](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cew70wjvv9do)</sup> Ukrainian President Zelensky condemned the assault as a deliberate "Easter escalation," framing the barrage as an unambiguous rejection by Moscow of peace negotiations at a moment when a temporary ceasefire had seemed within reach.<sup>[4](http://www.euronews.com/2026/04/04/eight-dead-in-massive-russian-attack-while-kyiv-signals-readiness-for-easter-ceasefire)</sup> The strikes killed at least eight civilians across multiple regions, with a single Russian drone hitting a covered market in Nikopol, killing five people and wounding 19 others in one of the deadliest individual strikes of the attack.<sup>[2](https://www.france24.com/en/europe/20260404-russian-drone-strike-on-ukraine-market-kills-five-wounds-19)</sup> Separate overnight bombardments wounded at least 14 additional people across the country as the volume of incoming ordnance strained Ukrainian air defences in several areas.<sup>[3](https://www.france24.com/en/war-in-ukraine-russian-night-time-strikes-wound-at-least-14-people)</sup>

Despite the scale of the attack, Kyiv signalled its continued readiness for an Easter ceasefire even as it launched retaliatory drone strikes deep into Russian territory, underlining the dual diplomatic and military track that has defined this phase of the conflict.<sup>[4](http://www.euronews.com/2026/04/04/eight-dead-in-massive-russian-attack-while-kyiv-signals-readiness-for-easter-ceasefire)</sup> European governments are closely monitoring the escalation, and the assault is expected to intensify Western diplomatic pressure on Moscow alongside renewed calls for increased military support to Ukraine.<sup>[1](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cew70wjvv9do)</sup><sup>[5](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cew70wjvv9do?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss)</sup> The episode underscores the fragility of any prospective ceasefire arrangement and the sustained civilian toll that Russian bombardment campaigns continue to inflict on Ukrainian communities.
top-story-2:
Storm Dave, the fourth named storm of the 2025-26 season, struck Ireland over the Easter weekend with some of the most severe wind conditions seen this spring, battering the island and extending disruption into parts of the UK.<sup>[1](https://www.rte.ie/news/ireland/2026/0404/1566697-storm-dave-ireland/)</sup> Met Eireann forecast gusts reaching up to 100 kilometres per hour across Ireland, with exposed coastal areas expected to experience even stronger gales.<sup>[3](https://www.irishtimes.com/environment/2026/04/04/met-eireann-forecasts-100kmh-gusts-and-rain-on-easter-weekend-as-storm-dave-nears-ireland/)</sup> A Status Yellow wind warning covering the entire country came into effect at 1pm on Saturday and was set to remain in force until 2am on Sunday, while additional yellow rain warnings were issued for Donegal and Mayo.<sup>[2](https://www.thejournal.ie/weather-warning-storm-dave-wind-7003448-Apr2026/)</sup> Emergency services and local authorities urged the public to secure outdoor items and exercise particular caution while travelling, with transport networks facing significant disruption across roads, rail, and air services.<sup>[4](https://www.irishexaminer.com/news/arid-41822090.html)</sup>

Coastal communities were placed on alert for flooding and erosion as the system pushed through, and rural areas faced the prospect of power outages as high winds affected overhead infrastructure.<sup>[1](https://www.rte.ie/news/ireland/2026/0404/1566697-storm-dave-ireland/)</sup> Across the Irish Sea, power company SSEN forecast gusts of approximately 80 miles per hour in some areas, prompting the Met Office to issue its own weather warnings for affected parts of the UK.<sup>[5](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c5y80nj5v50o)</sup> Both Irish and British meteorological services were tracking the system closely and indicated they would issue updated guidance as conditions developed throughout the Easter holiday period.<sup>[3](https://www.irishtimes.com/environment/2026/04/04/met-eireann-forecasts-100kmh-gusts-and-rain-on-easter-weekend-as-storm-dave-nears-ireland/)</sup>
top-story-3:
The US-Israeli military campaign against Iran entered its 36th day on Saturday with a significant escalation, as Iran claimed responsibility for shooting down a US warplane with one crew member remaining unaccounted for.<sup>[2](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/4/4/iran-war-what-is-happening-on-day-36-of-us-israeli-attacks?traffic_source=rss)</sup> Search and rescue operations were underway for the missing American service member, with former US military officials warning publicly about the serious dangers facing any recovery teams operating in hostile Iranian territory.<sup>[1](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiqgFBVV95cUxPTkU0aFRMbzRTckJCMzE1T09NZWFPWXNtOW1kNVM5RzRKZlFCOVl1UVlwZU5fLU5nTDRkbE82WU1EeEVLZjg0NHAyajZSdzNOWlhTc1FQNk9iNWpfbTBGWkFKT1ZueUhLUXQxbmVmMzUzLTlFOHFWeXR4bHJWWjFJa1NadU1PcS12anppMTJVaU9jWWpKZm9OQ19uVW9XMTZOZF9OSlZmS3BPUQ?oc=5)</sup> Iran also claimed to have downed a second US aircraft in recent operations, though those details remained contested.<sup>[4](https://www.npr.org/2026/04/04/nx-s1-5773436/iran-war-updates)</sup> The United States and Israel continued strikes targeting Iranian infrastructure in response, with petrochemical facilities and sites associated with the country's nuclear programme among the targets, raising sustained humanitarian concerns.<sup>[2](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/4/4/iran-war-what-is-happening-on-day-36-of-us-israeli-attacks?traffic_source=rss)</sup>

Israel simultaneously intensified its operations in Lebanon, adding a further theatre to an already sprawling regional conflict, while Iran's government executed two members of a banned opposition group on the same day as the latest military escalation — a convergence of domestic repression and international confrontation that drew sharp criticism from human rights observers.<sup>[3](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c0le43jw8e9o?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss)</sup><sup>[4](https://www.npr.org/2026/04/04/nx-s1-5773436/iran-war-updates)</sup> The incident involving the downed aircraft represents one of the most direct confrontations between US and Iranian forces since the conflict began, raising the prospect of further escalation at a moment when diplomatic channels appear increasingly strained.<sup>[1](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiqgFBVV95cUxPTkU0aFRMbzRTckJCMzE1T09NZWFPWXNtOW1kNVM5RzRKZlFCOVl1UVlwZU5fLU5nTDRkbE82WU1EeEVLZjg0NHAyajZSdzNOWlhTc1FQNk9iNWpfbTBGWkFKT1ZueUhLUXQxbmVmMzUzLTlFOHFWeXR4bHJWWjFJa1NadU1PcS12anppMTJVaU9jWWpKZm9OQ19uVW9XMTZOZF9OSlZmS3BPUQ?oc=5)</sup> The geopolitical ripple effects of the now six-week-old conflict extend well beyond the immediate region, contributing to elevated global energy prices and measurable economic pressure on nations from Southeast Asia to West Africa, with no near-term signs of resolution in sight.<sup>[2](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/4/4/iran-war-what-is-happening-on-day-36-of-us-israeli-attacks?traffic_source=rss)</sup>
world:
The two conflicts dominating global attention this Easter weekend are covered in the top stories above — Russia's massive drone and missile assault against Ukraine<sup>[1](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cew70wjvv9do)</sup><sup>[4](http://www.euronews.com/2026/04/04/eight-dead-in-massive-russian-attack-while-kyiv-signals-readiness-for-easter-ceasefire)</sup> and the US-Israeli campaign against Iran entering its 36th day with a US warplane shot down and a crew member missing.<sup>[6](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiqgFBVV95cUxPTkU0aFRMbzRTckJCMzE1T09NZWFPWXNtOW1kNVM5RzRKZlFCOVl1UVlwZU5fLU5nTDRkbE82WU1EeEVLZjg0NHAyajZSdzNOWlhTc1FQNk9iNWpfbTBGWkFKT1ZueUhLUXQxbmVmMzUzLTlFOHFWeXR4bHJWWjFJa1NadU1PcS12anppMTJVaU9jWWpKZm9OQ19uVW9XMTZOZF9OSlZmS3BPUQ?oc=5)</sup><sup>[7](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/4/4/iran-war-what-is-happening-on-day-36-of-us-israeli-attacks?traffic_source=rss)</sup> Within that Iran conflict, a parallel story of domestic repression is unfolding: Iran executed Abolhassan Montazer and Vahid Baniamerian, two convicted members of a banned opposition group, on the same day as its military operations — executions that drew international condemnation from human rights organisations monitoring Iran's treatment of dissidents during wartime.<sup>[14](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/4/4/iran-executes-suspected-members-of-dissident-group-amid-us-israel-war?traffic_source=rss)</sup>

Beyond the conflict zones, one of the day's most striking developments came from space. NASA's Artemis II mission reached the halfway point of its lunar journey, with Commander Reid Wiseman and the crew capturing spectacular images of Earth from the Orion capsule as they travelled deeper into space.<sup>[10](https://www.aljazeera.com/video/newsfeed/2026/4/4/nasa-unveils-first-images-of-earth-from-artemis-ii?traffic_source=rss)</sup><sup>[11](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/ce8jzr423p9o?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss)</sup> The milestone marks a significant step in humanity's renewed effort to return to the Moon, with the successful progress of the mission generating fresh enthusiasm for deep-space exploration across the international scientific community.<sup>[12](https://www.npr.org/2026/04/04/nx-s1-5764829/opinion-humanitys-hopes-ascended-with-artemis-ii)</sup> On the same day, a devastating earthquake in Afghanistan claimed the lives of eight members of a single family, with only a young child surviving, a tragedy that illustrated once again the acute vulnerability of Afghan communities to natural disasters in a country where ongoing instability and limited infrastructure complicate every rescue effort.<sup>[13](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/4/4/afghanistan-earthquake-kills-eight-members-of-same-family?traffic_source=rss)</sup>

The economic consequences of the Iran-US conflict are being felt far beyond the Middle East. Vietnam's first-quarter growth slowed measurably as rising energy costs driven by Gulf market volatility increased input costs for manufacturers and eroded export competitiveness, putting the government's 2026 full-year growth targets under pressure.<sup>[15](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMioAFBVV95cUxNSGx1Z0NnZXo4UzIxcGpSRXl6ZF95X00wdW9ZcTNYQkczVGJ3YkpZZk5aOGl0UExTaE92OEdZM09qa1l2SXM0VG9Oek1SSGJqbElIUnBkWC1TajNraUstb0pvZ2t0Vy00dGpEbjRTWVMxQVNrZmJNQWlmT25RSXZIRnV6bHFUT2k3NHBKZktUSmQ3dTIzZnZaQUFkRFAyUF9z?oc=5)</sup> Senegal presented an even starker picture: with fuel costs nearly doubling what the government had budgeted, Dakar imposed restrictions on ministerial foreign travel — a symbolic but telling measure of the fiscal pressure being transmitted from the Gulf to West Africa's developing economies.<sup>[16](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cp84z5r4d32o?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss)</sup> Both cases illustrate how sustained military conflict around the Persian Gulf propagates economic hardship across nations with no direct stake in the fighting.
europe:
The defining security story for Europe this Easter is Russia's massive bombardment of Ukraine, covered in detail in the top stories above — an assault that killed at least eight civilians, strained Ukrainian air defences, and prompted renewed calls across European capitals for intensified military and diplomatic support for Kyiv.<sup>[1](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cew70wjvv9do)</sup><sup>[4](http://www.euronews.com/2026/04/04/eight-dead-in-massive-russian-attack-while-kyiv-signals-readiness-for-easter-ceasefire)</sup> The conflict also reached further west than the primary front lines: a French investigative report revealed that more than 200 Ukrainian military specialists have been operating in Libya, where they are alleged to have used a maritime drone to strike the Russian gas carrier Arctic Metagaz.<sup>[14](http://www.euronews.com/2026/04/04/rfi-investigation-ukrainian-military-in-libya-and-the-strike-on-the-russian-gas-carrier-ar)</sup> The findings raise significant questions about the geographic scope of Ukrainian military operations and the implications for regional stability in North Africa, where European governments maintain complex interests.

At home, European households were contending with a sharply more expensive Easter table. Egg prices surged across the continent ahead of the holiday, with inflation in the category running well ahead of overall consumer price growth in most member states.<sup>[11](http://www.euronews.com/business/2026/04/04/egg-prices-surge-in-europe-ahead-of-easter-which-countries-saw-the-highest-rise)</sup> Climate-driven extreme weather has emerged as farmers' most pressing challenge, driving up production costs and constraining supply of the lamb and eggs central to Easter traditions — a direct transmission of environmental disruption into household budgets.<sup>[12](http://www.euronews.com/2026/04/04/the-cost-of-climate-change-is-hitting-homes-this-easter-your-lamb-roast-dinner-is-proof)</sup> In response to the broader energy price environment feeding this inflation, ministers of economy and finance from Italy, Germany, Spain, Portugal, and Austria sent a joint letter to the European Union calling for a windfall tax on energy sector surplus profits — a coordinated signal from five of the bloc's largest economies that pressure for institutional intervention in energy pricing is intensifying.<sup>[13](http://www.euronews.com/my-europe/2026/04/04/letter-to-eu-from-five-member-states-tax-extra-profits-of-energy-companies)</sup>

Storm Dave's impact across Ireland and the UK — covered in the top stories above — was the most immediate weather event affecting western Europe this weekend, though its disruption to Easter travel plans was at least foreseeable thanks to modern meteorological warning systems.<sup>[6](https://www.rte.ie/news/ireland/2026/0404/1566697-storm-dave-ireland/)</sup><sup>[10](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c5y80nj5v50o)</sup> In Italy, law enforcement reported a notable success with the arrest of Roberto Mazzarella, a fugitive mafia boss who had spent more than a year evading capture while living under a false identity at a luxury villa on the Amalfi Coast.<sup>[15](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cn437qkk80lo)</sup> Mazzarella was wanted for murder, and his apprehension underscores the sustained intelligence-gathering and operational coordination that Italian authorities have deployed against organised crime in southern Europe. Across the Channel, the UK was grappling with a rather different retail crime wave: organised gangs are reportedly travelling between towns to raid supermarket chocolate shelves, prompting retailers to introduce security measures for confectionery sections as rising cocoa prices made the commodity an increasingly attractive target for theft.<sup>[16](https://www.france24.com/en/in-the-uk-thieves-travel-around-the-country-to-raid-chocolate-shelves)</sup>
ireland:
Storm Dave dominated the start of Easter weekend in Ireland, with a nationwide Status Yellow wind warning in force from 1pm Saturday to 2am Sunday as Met Eireann forecast gusts of up to 100 kilometres per hour and even stronger gales along exposed coastlines.<sup>[1](https://www.rte.ie/news/ireland/2026/0404/1566697-storm-dave-ireland/)</sup><sup>[3](https://www.irishtimes.com/environment/2026/04/04/met-eireann-forecasts-100kmh-gusts-and-rain-on-easter-weekend-as-storm-dave-nears-ireland/)</sup> Additional yellow rain warnings were issued for Donegal and Mayo, transport services faced disruption, coastal communities were on alert for flooding and erosion, and rural households prepared for potential power outages as the fourth named storm of the season pushed through.<sup>[2](https://www.thejournal.ie/weather-warning-storm-dave-wind-7003448-Apr2026/)</sup><sup>[4](https://www.irishexaminer.com/news/arid-41822090.html)</sup> Emergency services urged the public to exercise caution, particularly on roads and near the coast, throughout the holiday period.<sup>[5](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c5y80nj5v50o)</sup>

While Storm Dave was the most immediate concern at home, a serious overseas development was drawing sustained attention from Dublin. More than 300 Irish Defence Force soldiers at Camp Shamrock in southern Lebanon now find themselves positioned behind Israeli Defence Force lines following Israel's military advance into the region, raising acute concerns about the safety of Irish personnel serving with the UN peacekeeping mission UNIFIL.<sup>[6](https://www.irishtimes.com/ireland/2026/04/04/irish-peacekeepers-in-lebanon-now-behind-israeli-lines-as-idf-advances-south/)</sup> Ireland's Minister for Defence expressed serious concern about the soldiers' security as the IDF expanded its operations, with diplomatic efforts reportedly underway to ensure protection for the Irish contingent — a development with direct implications for Ireland's long-standing participation in international peacekeeping.

The Easter weekend also brought a moment of formal historical reckoning, as Ireland held official commemorations of the 1916 Easter Rising, with government representatives attending ceremonies at the Garden of Remembrance to honour those whose actions set Ireland on the path to independence.<sup>[9](https://www.rte.ie/news/ireland/2026/0404/1566730-easter-rising-commemoration/)</sup> A different chapter of contested Irish history resurfaced as well: the widow of Denis Donaldson, the former Sinn Féin official who was exposed as a long-serving British agent before his murder, renewed her call for a public inquiry into the full circumstances of his death, citing unresolved questions about accountability and state transparency.<sup>[10](https://www.rte.ie/news/ulster/2026/0404/1566652-denis-donaldson-widow/)</sup> Separately, Ireland's first substantive public hearing on the government's Covid-19 pandemic response took place, with testimony from officials shedding light on decision-making, public health coordination, and the handling of restrictions — a process aimed at establishing a comprehensive record to inform future emergency preparedness.<sup>[7](https://www.rte.ie/news/analysis-and-comment/2026/0404/1566623-covid-review-analysis/)</sup>

On the economic front, the government unveiled a new SSIA-style savings initiative designed to unlock household savings and encourage greater financial participation, drawing on the template of the highly popular Special Savings Investment Account scheme from the Celtic Tiger era.<sup>[8](https://www.irishexaminer.com/news/spotlight/arid-41821202.html)</sup> The plan offers government matching contributions to incentivise saving among households, and officials positioned it as a tool for building financial resilience while supporting broader economic growth. Law enforcement made headlines for different reasons: four individuals appeared in court charged with offences linked to a seizure of approximately €7 million worth of cannabis in County Kildare, one of the more substantial drug busts of recent months.<sup>[11](https://www.irishtimes.com/crime-law/courts/2026/04/04/four-in-court-charged-over-7m-cannabis-seizure-in-co-kildare/)</sup> At an Irish Cup semi-final in Belfast between Cliftonville and Dungannon Swifts, a child was struck by a bottle during disorder that broke out before the match, with police making at least one arrest — an incident that raised renewed concerns about spectator safety at cross-community sporting events.<sup>[12](https://www.irishtimes.com/crime-law/2026/04/04/child-hit-by-bottle-during-disorder-before-irish-cup-semi-final-in-belfast/)</sup>
